prompt: Complete the diary entry with the missing particle for each phrasal verb (turn over, read out, look up, rub out, cross out, rip up, write down, point out).
explanation: Each gap completes a phrasal verb from the unit: turn (papers) over, read out instructions, look up words, rub out / cross out / rip up a mistake, write your name down, and point out a fact.
passageTemplate:
24th June

We had our English exam today! It was a disaster! We all sat there nervously, waiting for Mrs Jennings to say we could start. Finally, she told us to turn our exam papers {{b1}}. Then she read {{b2}} the instructions to make sure we all understood.

We had to write three essays in two hours! We weren't allowed to look {{b3}} any words in the dictionary, and we had to write in pen. That meant we couldn't rub anything {{b4}} if we made a mistake. We had to cross it {{b5}} neatly or just rip {{b6}} the whole piece of paper and start again.

So, I read through the three questions very carefully and thought about what I was going to write. I'd just written my name {{b7}} at the top of the first piece of paper, and was about to start writing the first essay, when Mrs Jennings pointed {{b8}} that there were only five minutes left. Oh dear!
